sae_features.label: Tetraspan 4-TM membrane proteins
sae_features.description: Signature of small four-pass (tetraspan) alpha-helical membrane proteins: the model recognizes the canonical 4-TM bundle and short connecting loops, especially the central TM2–TM3 pair, as found in MARVEL-domain proteins, the claudin/PMP22/EMP superfamily, MS4A, LHFPL, and CaVγ/TARP-like subunits; the signal reflects hydrophobic TM helices with helix-boundary aromatics and small-residue (Gly/Ala, often Cys) packing motifs

sae_features.label: Multipass cytosolic tail IDRs
sae_features.description: Intrinsically disordered terminal tails (N- and/or C-termini) of multi-pass membrane proteins—most commonly four-pass/tetraspan-like families (claudins, MARVEL/synaptogyrins, MS4A/CD20-like, tetraspanins, LHFPL, GSG1L) and analogous tails of viral multi-pass glycoproteins—serving as scaffolding/regulatory segments (e.g., PDZ-binding, phosphorylation). The transmembrane core and structured loops are largely ignored.

sae_features.label: Glycosylated extracellular loops and N-termini
sae_features.description: Extracellular/lumenal N-termini and interhelical loops of multi-pass eukaryotic membrane proteins—flexible, disordered segments at transmembrane-helix boundaries that are enriched for N-linked glycosylation sequons and disulfide-forming cysteines (common in class A GPCRs, ion channels, and solute transporters).

sae_features.label: N-terminal TM1–ECL1–TM2 hairpin
sae_features.description: N-terminal helical submodule of small eukaryotic multi-pass membrane proteins—typically the TM1–ECL1–TM2 hairpin and often extending into the adjacent helix (TM3) or the pre‑TM1 cytosolic helix—characterized by helix–helix packing small-residue motifs, interfacial aromatics (Trp/Tyr), and cysteine-rich, sometimes glycosylated first extracellular/lumenal loops; most common in 4‑TM families (claudins, tetraspanins, MS4A, MARVEL/MAL, synaptophysin/synaptoporin, SCAMP) and related ER/peroxisomal enzymes.

sae_features.label: Multi-pass transmembrane helices
sae_features.description: Eukaryotic polytopic (multi-pass) alpha-helical membrane regions of secretory/endomembrane proteins—transporters, scaffolds, and membrane-embedded enzymes—with signal concentrated on the transmembrane helix bundle and the immediately adjacent membrane-proximal loops/tails, rather than on soluble or catalytic domains.

sae_features.label: Disulfide- and glycan-decorated extracellular loops
sae_features.description: Disulfide- and glycan-decorated extracellular loop modules of small multi-pass membrane proteins, especially 4‑TM families. The feature captures the long loops between transmembrane helices (most often ECL1 or the large EC2) that are cysteine‑rich (frequent CC/CCG), often N‑glycosylated, and patterned with aromatic/Gly/Pro residues; it generalizes to analogous non‑TM loops in related channels/subunits (e.g., K2P, gap-junction proteins) and can occasionally extend to long cytosolic loops/tails within the same architectures.

sae_features.label: Transmembrane helix entry detector
sae_features.description: Transmembrane α-helix entry/boundary detector in multi-pass membrane proteins—especially four-pass/tetraspan-like families—recognizing the loop-to-helix transition at the N-terminal end of TM segments enriched in small residues (Gly/Ala/Ser/Thr), glycine-zipper (GxxxG) helix-packing patterns, and interfacial aromatics (Trp/Tyr)

sae_features.label: Cytosolic-biased helix-loop boundaries
sae_features.description: Helix–loop boundary elements of multi-pass α-helical membrane proteins: the termini of transmembrane helices and their short connecting loops, with a bias toward cytosolic sides and positive-inside/helix-capping motifs, but also detectable on some extracellular loops.
